title: CBIC Streamlines Procedures for Export Cargo Returned Due to Strait of Hormuz Disruption
content:
# CBIC Streamlines Procedures for Export Cargo Returned Due to Strait of Hormuz Disruption

Ek important update hai! The Central Board of Indirect Taxes and Customs (CBIC) ne export cargo containers ke liye ek simplified procedure announce kiya hai, jo Strait of Hormuz mein disruption ke karan India wapas aa gaye hain. This is a welcome move to ease the challenges faced by exporters due to the current maritime situation.

Here's a quick lowdown on what's been announced:

*   **Circular Details:** This procedure has been issued vide Circular No. 21/2026-Customs dated April 15, 2026.
*   **Purpose:** The circular provides a clear process for handling export cargo containers that were off-loaded at foreign ports and subsequently returned to India because of the disruptions in maritime routes.
*   **Key Procedures:**
    *   A fresh Shipping Arrival Manifest (SAM) will need to be filed.
    *   Container and seal integrity will be verified.
    *   If seals are found intact, containers can be offloaded without filing a Bill of Entry.
    *   Shipping Bills can be cancelled through the EDI system, and "back to town" procedures may be followed for such goods.
*   **In case of Tampered Seals:** If container seals are found tampered with or not intact, 100% examination will be mandated, and re-import procedures will apply.
*   **Recovery of Incentives:** Authorities will also ensure the recovery of any export incentives, such as IGST refunds and drawback, if they have already been disbursed for the returned cargo.
*   **Validity:** This relaxation is temporary and is valid until April 30, 2026.

This measure aims to facilitate trade and ensure expeditious handling of affected cargo during these exceptional circumstances.

## Read the Official Document
*   [Download the Official Notification/Circular here](https://www.cbic.gov.in)

*Published on: 16 April 2026*
